questions.stem: ___ (AF) is the most common sustained cardiac arrhythmia, affecting approximately 2-4% of the adult population. Prevalence increases significantly with age, reaching 5-8% in individuals over 65 years.
questions.source_quote: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is the most common sustained cardiac arrhythmia, affecting approximately 2-4% of the adult population. Prevalence increases significantly with age, reaching 5-8% in individuals over 65 years.
questions.explanation: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is the most common sustained cardiac arrhythmia, affecting approximately 2-4% of the adult population. Prevalence increases significantly with age, reaching 5-8% in individuals over 65 years.
Hypertension: Present in ~60% of AF patients.
Obesity: BMI >30 increases AF risk by 49%.

questions.stem: The ___ score is the standard tool for stroke risk stratification:
questions.source_quote: The **CHA₂DS₂-VASc** score is the standard tool for stroke risk stratification:
questions.explanation: The CHA₂DS₂-VASc score is the standard tool for stroke risk stratification:

questions.stem: ___ (apixaban, rivaroxaban, edoxaban, dabigatran) are preferred over warfarin.
questions.source_quote: **NOACs** (apixaban, rivaroxaban, edoxaban, dabigatran) are preferred over warfarin.
questions.explanation: NOACs (apixaban, rivaroxaban, edoxaban, dabigatran) are preferred over warfarin.

questions.stem: ___ is no longer recommended for stroke prevention in AF.
questions.source_quote: **Aspirin alone** is no longer recommended for stroke prevention in AF.
questions.explanation: Aspirin alone is no longer recommended for stroke prevention in AF.

questions.source_quote: Rate vs rhythm control strategy should be individualized.
questions.explanation: Rate vs rhythm control strategy should be individualized.
